title: Hand on belly: bills are met
subtitle: A three-minute embodiment practice for feeling the truth of bills being paid in the body rather than the head.
excerpt: A three-minute embodiment practice for the moment after paying a bill. When the head says the bill is paid but the body hasn't caught up. One hand flat on the belly, one slow breath, one sentence said to the body: the bills are met. Use after a payment confirms, when relief is the right response but tension is still running.
sourceNotes: Belly-hand grounding as a somatic anchor is documented across the somatic-therapy lineage (Peter Levine's Somatic Experiencing, Pat Ogden's Sensorimotor Psychotherapy). The bills-are-met application is adapted from Day 7 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025), "Invite peace around paying bills."

content.text: A three-minute embodiment practice for after a bill payment. When the head knows the bill is paid but the body is still running the alert. One hand flat on the belly, one slow breath, one sentence said to the body rather than the mind. The payment went through; the body is allowed to know it.

Close the laptop or put the phone down. Sit with both feet flat on the floor. Place one hand, either hand, flat on the centre of the belly, just below the navel. Feel the weight of the hand against the body.
Take one slow in-breath, feeling the belly rise slightly under the hand. On the out-breath, say, out loud or in your mind:

attrs.quote: The bills are met. I am met.

content.text: Say it three times, one per out-breath. The hand stays warm on the belly throughout. The belly is the body's centre of weight and safety, the hand on it tells the body the threat is over, the payment is done.
Let the hand stay for one slow minute longer. Notice the breath under the hand. When you take the hand away, the warmth stays. The practice is landed.

Putting a hand on the belly as a calming anchor has a long history in somatic-therapy practice. The body holds steady ground when the head is busy. The bills-are-met version is adapted from a tapping programme on money. See the Sources note below for the books.
